title: Immanuel part 2
scripture_text: “The virgin will conceive and give birth to a son, and they will call him Immanuel” (which means “God with us”).
devotional:
Look carefully at what the angel said to Joseph, ‘You shall call His name Jesus, Because he will save his people from their sins.’ This would have been mind blowing for Joseph. Nothing had ever been said like this before.

Let me explain, being a Jew, Joseph would have been taught the importance of going to the temple at least three times a year to make sacrifice for the forgiveness of sin, as well as for other things.

These sacrifices for forgiveness were extremely limited in two ways. Firstly, they were only a sign post to God’s ultimate sacrifice in Jesus. But secondly, although the animal sacrifice assured the person making them of forgiveness, they were unable to save them from their sins i.e. the power of sin.

When we read ‘You shall call His name Jesus Because he will save his people from their sins.’ we automatically assume that this is speaking forgiveness. This statement includes forgiveness, but it is a lot more. Jesus wants to break the power or control of sin in our lives so we can enjoy Immanuel.

Paul wrote in Romans 6:14 ‘’For sin shall no longer be your master, because you are not under the law, but under grace.’’

If God is with us, then how do you explain all the crazy stuff that is happening in our lives? That is a very good and important question.

When you read that the angel told Joseph that the child would be called Immanuel, Joseph was in the middle of a crisis. This was a time of emotional confusion, uncertainty and fear. Mary, his betrothed, was pregnant outside marriage, she claimed that God was the father, which which would have been perceived as blasphemy. This was far from being an ideal situation. And yet, it was here that Joseph learned about the glory of Immanuel.

The life of Joseph teaches us that our need for ‘Immanuel.’ God with us, will always be greater than our need for a change of our circumstances. When life is very difficult and painful sometimes God will come and change our circumstances, but very often he wont, because he understands that experiencing him with us in the crisis is what we really need.

‘To stand in the presence of God, that is what the gospel is. The Gospel is not primarily about forgiveness. It is not primarily about good feelings. It is not primarily about power. All these things are by-products, sparks. It’s primarily about the presence of God.’ Timothy Keller.

https://quotefancy.com/quote/921425/Timothy-Keller-To-stand-in-the-presence-of-God-that-is-what-the-Gospel-is-The-Gospel-is accessed 2/04/2025

“Why do some persons 'find' God in a way that others do not? Why does God manifest His Presence to some and let multitudes of others struggle along in the half-light of imperfect Christian experience? Of course the will of God is the same for all. He has no favourites within His household. All He has ever done for any of His children He will do for all of His children. The difference lies not with God but with us.― A.W. Tozer, https://www.goodreads.com/quotes/1112562-why-do-some-persons-find-god-in-a-way-that accessed 2/04/2025

We have been created and designed for divine immanence, that is why Jesus is Immanuel. We need to teach ourselves that although the removal of difficult circumstances is rightfully desirable, our greater need is the intimate presence of God.

At times, the wounds of life are so deep and so acute that the only way we can fully recover, is to have our hearts and minds marinated in the healing presence of God. Immanuel comes to us in the normality of everyday life. When you think about it, the circumstances that brought Immanuel into the world were not very spectacular or impressive. There was no red carpet, no welcoming dignitaries, no formal civic reception, no grandiose event to receive Immanuel. Just a couple who were ordinary, a couple who had no power, position or prestige. A young girl who had conceived outside marriage giving birth in a strange place far from home. This is the way it had to be, because Immanuel wants to be God with us in the ordinariness of our everyday lives. Immanuel with us in our ordinariness is the real glory of life. In our homes, in our marriages, in our families, in all our relationships, at our work, in our lack of work, in our school, in our university course, in our health, in our sickness, in the good times, in the bad times and in every other situation and circumstance of our lives, is where Immanuel longs to be.
prayer: Father I need to know Immanuel in all of my life.
html:
Immanuel part 2

Study Text: Matthew 1:23
“The virgin will conceive and give birth to a son, and they will call him Immanuel” (which means “God with us”).

Look carefully at what the angel said to Joseph, ‘You shall call His name Jesus, Because he will save his people from their sins.’ This would have been mind blowing for Joseph. Nothing had ever been said like this before.

Let me explain, being a Jew, Joseph would have been taught the importance of going to the temple at least three times a year to make sacrifice for the forgiveness of sin, as well as for other things.

These sacrifices for forgiveness were extremely limited in two ways. Firstly, they were only a sign post to God’s ultimate sacrifice in Jesus. But secondly, although the animal sacrifice assured the person making them of forgiveness, they were unable to save them from their sins i.e. the power of sin.

When we read ‘You shall call His name Jesus Because he will save his people from their sins.’ we automatically assume that this is speaking forgiveness. This statement includes forgiveness, but it is a lot more. Jesus wants to break the power or control of sin in our lives so we can enjoy Immanuel.

Paul wrote in Romans 6:14 ‘’For sin shall no longer be your master, because you are not under the law, but under grace.’’

If God is with us, then how do you explain all the crazy stuff that is happening in our lives? That is a very good and important question.

When you read that the angel told Joseph that the child would be called Immanuel, Joseph was in the middle of a crisis. This was a time of emotional confusion, uncertainty and fear. Mary, his betrothed, was pregnant outside marriage, she claimed that God was the father, which which would have been perceived as blasphemy. This was far from being an ideal situation. And yet, it was here that Joseph learned about the glory of Immanuel.

The life of Joseph teaches us that our need for ‘Immanuel.’ God with us, will always be greater than our need for a change of our circumstances. When life is very difficult and painful sometimes God will come and change our circumstances, but very often he wont, because he understands that experiencing him with us in the crisis is what we really need.

‘To stand in the presence of God, that is what the gospel is. The Gospel is not primarily about forgiveness. It is not primarily about good feelings. It is not primarily about power. All these things are by-products, sparks. It’s primarily about the presence of God.’ Timothy Keller.

https://quotefancy.com/quote/921425/Timothy-Keller-To-stand-in-the-presence-of-God-that-is-what-the-Gospel-is-The-Gospel-is accessed 2/04/2025

“Why do some persons 'find' God in a way that others do not? Why does God manifest His Presence to some and let multitudes of others struggle along in the half-light of imperfect Christian experience? Of course the will of God is the same for all. He has no favourites within His household. All He has ever done for any of His children He will do for all of His children. The difference lies not with God but with us.― A.W. Tozer, https://www.goodreads.com/quotes/1112562-why-do-some-persons-find-god-in-a-way-that accessed 2/04/2025

We have been created and designed for divine immanence, that is why Jesus is Immanuel. We need to teach ourselves that although the removal of difficult circumstances is rightfully desirable, our greater need is the intimate presence of God.

At times, the wounds of life are so deep and so acute that the only way we can fully recover, is to have our hearts and minds marinated in the healing presence of God. Immanuel comes to us in the normality of everyday life. When you think about it, the circumstances that brought Immanuel into the world were not very spectacular or impressive. There was no red carpet, no welcoming dignitaries, no formal civic reception, no grandiose event to receive Immanuel. Just a couple who were ordinary, a couple who had no power, position or prestige. A young girl who had conceived outside marriage giving birth in a strange place far from home. This is the way it had to be, because Immanuel wants to be God with us in the ordinariness of our everyday lives. Immanuel with us in our ordinariness is the real glory of life. In our homes, in our marriages, in our families, in all our relationships, at our work, in our lack of work, in our school, in our university course, in our health, in our sickness, in the good times, in the bad times and in every other situation and circumstance of our lives, is where Immanuel longs to be.

Prayer: Father I need to know Immanuel in all of my life.
Bible in a year reading plan: - Job 25-28
